title: Little Lives
one_line: A tiny 3D life sim: keep one Sim's needs met by directing them around a single room.

reference_grounding.primary: The Sims (Maxis/EA) — the motives/needs system and the mix of Sim free-will decay with direct player commands.
borrowed: Continuous needs (motives) that decay over real time and are refilled by interacting with furniture.
Player issues an interaction command; the Sim autonomously walks to the object and performs it.
Mood is a readable summary of how well needs are met.
explicitly_not_in_slice: Build/Buy mode, placing furniture, multiple rooms or lots.
Multiple Sims, relationships, social needs, careers, aging.
Inventory economy / shopping (Simoleons shown as flavor only).

player_verbs: Orbit and zoom the 3D camera (drag to rotate, wheel to zoom) to read the room.
Direct the Sim to a piece of furniture (click its on-screen action button or press 1-4).
Triage: choose which decaying need to address next.
rules: Four needs — Energy, Hunger, Fun, Hygiene — start partly filled (0..100) and decay continuously at different rates.
Each of four furniture pieces restores exactly one need: Bed->Energy, Fridge->Hunger, Sofa/TV->Fun, Shower->Hygiene.
Directing the Sim sets a target; the Sim walks there, then 'uses' it for a few seconds while the matching need refills.
Only one interaction at a time; a new command interrupts and re-routes.
Mood = average of the four needs. A need at/near zero is a visible red crisis.
feedback: Four color-coded need bars (green -> amber -> red) update live.
The Sim visibly walks toward the target and plays a 'using' bob while refilling.
Mood readout (color + value) reflects the average; the lowest need is highlighted as the suggested next action.
Day clock advances; Simoleons counter shown as flavor.
risk: Needs decay while the Sim walks/uses, so addressing one need lets others fall.
Letting a need hit zero tanks mood — the player must keep triaging.

goals.short_term: Raise the currently-lowest need before it reaches crisis.
goals.session: Keep all four needs out of the red and end the day with a good (green) mood.

replay_reason: Different decay rates and starting values force a different triage order each run; the day cycle gives a natural session boundary.

first_screen.one_location: A single furnished room rendered in 3D.
first_screen.primary_path: Read the need bars, pick the lowest, send the Sim to the matching furniture.
first_screen.next_action: Lowest need is highlighted; its furniture button is the call to action.
first_screen.visible_progress: Need bars rise during use; mood value climbs; interaction count increments.
first_screen.locks: Cannot start a second interaction until the current walk/use finishes (or is re-commanded).

acceptance_checks: Scene renders a 3D room with floor/walls, four distinct furniture pieces, and a Sim.
Needs decay over time and are observable via DevAPI game.state.
Commanding furniture (key 1-4 / button) moves the Sim to it and refills the matching need.
Mood equals the average of the four needs and updates live.
